prevalence.value: 5-20% (older adults; higher in frail and autonomic disease)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Drop in SBP ≥20 mmHg or DBP ≥10 mmHg within 3 minutes of standing; evaluate volume status, medications, autonomic causes

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; improves with medication review and non-pharmacologic measures

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often missed unless measured; APS can implement orthostatic vitals in high-risk patients
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Hydration, slow position changes, compression stockings, adjust medications, treat anemia; pharmacologic therapy in specialist care when needed
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Polypharmacy and limited time for comprehensive geriatric review
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Many effective interventions are low-cost and feasible in primary care
